races.name: Tour de Romandie – Stage 1
races.finish_type: Flat sprint on valley floor — 33.8km after summit of Ovronnaz
races.description: The first road stage of the 2026 Tour de Romandie, and the first real test for the GC. Starting and finishing in Martigny in the Rhône Valley, the 171.2km route begins with three opening circuits of 37.4km each, featuring the short but savage Côte de La Rasse (2.5km @ 8.4%, Cat.3) on every lap. After the third circuit, the race pivots north-east along the flat Rhône floor to Saxon, where an intermediate sprint is held, before the road swings south up the Val d'Ardon to attack the Ovronnaz (8.9km @ 9.8%, Cat.1 — final 2.7km @ 10.4%). This is the decisive test of the day. The summit of Ovronnaz comes 33.8km from the finish — a long descent through the mountain village, then a flat 22km run back into Martigny. Because of this long run-in, the finish is genuinely open: a strong climber who attacks solo on Ovronnaz could hold it all the way, but a powerful reduced group could also sprint it out. Bonus seconds at finish: 10s/6s/4s. Weather forecast: sunny, ~20-26°C at race level, mild north-easterly wind of no consequence.

standings.notes: Few hundredths behind Söderqvist. Returned from foot fracture sustained in crash at Volta a Catalunya.
Best GC contender. Started last, delivered clean ride but couldn't match Godon. 7s deficit is very manageable.
BIG LOSER of the prologue — lost around 30 seconds to his rivals before the race had properly begun. GC ambitions severely compromised.

gc_after_prologue.prologue_notes: Dorian Godon (INEOS, French national champion) was a complete surprise winner of the prologue, covering 3.2km in 3:35 at 53.58 km/h — the fourth win of his 2026 season and 20th of his career, but his first in a time trial since 2019. He measured the crucial 800m uphill finale perfectly. Söderqvist and Oliveira were separated by a few hundredths at the line. Pogačar, starting last of 103 riders, rode cleanly but couldn't dent Godon's time even with the classification advantage of going last. Key context: Pogačar had been at LBL 48 hours earlier and said he had no idea where he stood condition-wise. His 7-second deficit is easily recoverable over five hilly stages.

key_climbs.name: Côte de La Rasse
key_climbs.notes: Tackled three times in the opening circuits. Sharp and punchy — could be used for attacks in later passes but more of a selection/breakaway test.

key_climbs.name: Saxon (intermediate sprint)
key_climbs.notes: Bonus sprint in the Rhône valley before the road turns towards Ovronnaz.

key_climbs.name: Ovronnaz
key_climbs.notes: The decisive climb of Stage 1. At 8.9km @ 9.8% avg, this is a genuine GC test — the final 2.7km averages 10.4%. However, the summit is 33.8km from the finish (descent + 22km flat), making a solo attack risky and a reduced bunch sprint viable. IDL: 'Pogačar can take 30-50 seconds here if he goes full gas — but then what? 33km is a long way home.' Expect either a Pogačar attack that sticks, or a small group (4-8 riders) contesting a reduced sprint in Martigny.

remaining_stages.type: Circuit with Ovronnaz Cat.1, downhill finish

remaining_stages.notes: Vuillens circuit (3.6km @ 4.8%). Final kicker to Vucherens inside 5km. Good for punchy sprinters.

remaining_stages.type: Circuit, Col de Mollendruz, downhill finish
remaining_stages.notes: Col de Mollendruz (9km @ 6.1%) then 21.4km descent to Orbe finish.
Queen stage by elevation. Jaunpass (8.1km @ 8.3%) twice + Saanenmöser. 16.7km descent to Charmey finish.
Only summit finish of the race. Leysin (13.9km @ 5.9%, max 12%). Final GC showdown. Race winner decided here.

races.rider_notes: IDL names Pogačar as Stage 1 top favourite, with Valentin Paret-Peintre (Soudal-Quick Step) and Lorenzo Fortunato (XDS Astana) as alternatives. Pogačar will likely test his rivals on Ovronnaz. The key question is whether he attacks solo and tries to hold the long descent and valley, or plays it safe in a group sprint. Given his GC position (7s deficit to Godon, 1s to Oliveira), he might push hard but not take extreme risks. Lipowitz and Roglič are the main Red Bull cards — they're only 10 and 8 seconds back respectively. Martinez is at +17s. Godon (the race leader) will be protected by INEOS but is not a threat over Ovronnaz — his lead will almost certainly evaporate today. Onley is 30 seconds down and effectively out of GC contention.

broadcasts.notes: Stage 1 on TNT Sports (main channel per Cyclist magazine schedule). Broadcast begins ~90 minutes before finish, so around 15:45-16:00 CEST (13:45-14:00 UTC). From £25.99/month.
Free live coverage on RTS Sport in Switzerland.
Eurosport 1 with English commentary across European markets, via discovery+ or HBO Max.
No Australian broadcaster holds Tour de Romandie rights. Cyclingnews confirmed: 'no Australian channels have picked up Tour de Romandie.' VPN to Swiss RTS or French L'Équipe is the only option.

data_confidence.notes: Distance 171.2km per PCS LiveStats and multiple sources (cyclingstage.com: 170.6km; domestique: 171.2km; PCS result page: 171.2km — using 171.2km). Elevation 2,186m per PCS LiveStats (domestique says 1,985m; cycling archives says 2,186m). Start time: IDL says 13:25, PCS LiveStats says 13:30 — using 13:25 per IDL preview ('Start: 1:25 p.m.'). Finish ~17:26 CEST per IDL. GC standings after prologue confirmed from PCS result page, AFP/France24, Cyclingnews, and multiple other sources. Söderqvist 2nd, Oliveira 3rd per PCS (some sources invert them — PCS authoritative). Pogačar 5th per The National and AFP; 6th per Cyclingnews — small discrepancy, using 5th. Onley ~30s loss confirmed by IDL. Broadcasts: UK TNT (Stage 1 on main TNT Sports channel per Cyclist magazine). Australia NO SBS confirmed by Cyclingnews.

races.name: Presidential Cycling Tour of Türkiye – Stage 4
races.finish_type: Flat final kilometre (0.4%) — sprint or reduced sprint after hilly stage
races.description: Stage 4 of the Tour of Türkiye follows yesterday's summit finish at Kiran with a 130.4km hilly transition stage from Marmaris to Fethiye, along the Turkish Riviera. The stage accumulates 1,637m of elevation but has no summit finish — a Cat.3 climb at km 93.5 (2km @ 6.9%) is the only classified ascent, followed by a long descent before a flat 1km finale into Fethiye (0.4% gradient). With GC leader Iván Sosa now 13 seconds ahead of Berwick and 21 seconds ahead of Breuillard, this stage is expected to see the sprinters' teams reassert control after the climbers' day yesterday. Tom Crabbe (Team Flanders-Baloise) won Stages 1 and 2 but dropped off the back on Stage 3's final climb — he remains the sprint benchmark if he can recover. The route's rolling Mediterranean terrain may still produce a reduced bunch rather than a full sprint.

standings.notes: Colombian climber, 28, first win since Tour de Langkawi 2022. Post iliac artery surgery comeback.

recent_results.notes: Crabbe's 3rd win of 2026. Messy sprint. Belgian 20-year-old.
Crabbe dropped at 10km from finish but fought back to win sprint.

First summit finish. Sosa attacked at 4.8km from top on 9km @ 9.7% Kiran climb. First win since 2022 Tour de Langkawi. Dedicated to Cristian Camilo Muñoz (Pogačar's late teammate who died that week). Sosa had endofibrosis surgery in 2025. TotalEnergies set tempo on the climb. Crabbe dropped off GC. Eurosport confirmed broadcaster.

broadcasts.notes: FloBikes has live-streamed all Tour of Türkiye stages free on their official YouTube channel. Stages 1, 2 and 3 all streamed on YouTube. Check the FloBikes YouTube channel on race day for the Stage 4 live link.
Eurosport confirmed as broadcaster for Stage 3 (Eurosport Spain tweeted stage coverage). StreamMax (Discovery/Eurosport streaming) also confirmed for Spain.
Full coverage with commentary on FloBikes.
TRT Sport provides domestic free coverage.

data_confidence.notes: Stage 4 Marmaris → Fethiye, 130.4km confirmed by Cyclingnews stages page and Spanish sources (esciclismo: 130.4km; infobae: 130.4km). Elevation 1,637m per infobae. Cat.3 climb at km 93.5 (2km @ 6.9%) confirmed by esciclismo route description. Finish gradient 0.4% (flat) confirmed. YouTube free stream confirmed for previous stages (1, 2, 3). Eurosport confirmed as Stage 3 broadcaster from Eurosport Spain tweet. Start time approximate.

podium: 1. Dorian Godon (FRA, INEOS Grenadiers) — +6s over field
2. Jakob Söderqvist (SWE, Lidl-Trek) — +6s, also wins Best Young Rider jersey
3. Ivo Oliveira (POR, UAE Team Emirates-XRG) — +6s (hundredths behind Söderqvist)
yesterday_results.notes: Huge surprise — Godon was not even among the top prologue favourites. He is the French national road champion and has 3 sprint wins in 2026 (Volta a Catalunya Stage 1, Paris-Nice shortened stage, others) but no TT wins since 2019. He judged the crucial 800m uphill ramp to perfection. Pogačar, going last, delivered a clean first time trial of 2026 but couldn't close the gap. Big loser: Oscar Onley (INEOS) lost ~30 seconds and is effectively out of GC contention. Roglič and Lipowitz both saved energy (Jan Tratnik also deliberately cruised). New Colnago TT2 time trial bike debuted for Pogačar at this race — lighter and more aerodynamic.

podium: 1. Iván Ramiro Sosa (COL, Equipo Kern Pharma) — solo win
yesterday_results.notes: Sosa's first win since Tour de Langkawi 2022 — a remarkable comeback following iliac artery endofibrosis surgery in 2025 (a vascular condition that restricts blood flow to the legs, career-threatening in cyclists). Now leads GC by 13s. Dedicated win to Cristian Camilo Muñoz, the Colombian cyclist who died that week after a crash at Tour du Jura. Pogačar also wore a black armband at LBL for Muñoz.

context.narrative: Two races unfolding simultaneously today across different terrains — the Swiss Alps and the Turkish Riviera. In Switzerland, the Tour de Romandie's real racing begins: Godon defends an unexpected yellow jersey but will almost certainly lose it on the Ovronnaz. The question is whether Pogačar goes hard enough to take 7+ seconds back and seize the lead himself — or whether he saves himself for the queen stages later in the week. In Turkey, Sosa attempts to defend his 13-second GC lead through a predominantly sprint-friendly stage where his rivals will try to eat into the deficit through echelons or a reduced bunch.

upcoming.notes: Rue → Vucherens, 173.1km. Rolling/attritional circuit, punchy uphill finish to Vucherens. Vuillens climb (3.6km @ 4.8%) x3, final ascent inside 5km. Puncheur day — could be good for the same type of riders who thrive at Flèche Wallonne.

upcoming.race: Tour de Romandie Stage 4 — Queen Stage (2.UWT)
upcoming.notes: Broc → Charmey, 149.6km, 3,175m climbing. Jaunpass (8.1km @ 8.3%) tackled twice from different sides plus Saanenmöser. Long descent to Charmey. Most likely GC-defining stage before Leysin.
Lucens → Leysin, 178.2km. Only summit finish of the race. Leysin: 13.9km @ 5.9% avg, max 12%. This decides the overall winner. Pogačar the heavy favourite.

issues: Stage 1 distance: PCS result page shows 171.2km; cyclingstage.com says 170.6km; domestique says 171.2km. Using 171.2km per PCS.
Stage 1 elevation: PCS shows 2,186m on LiveStats; domestique says 1,985m; cycling archives says 2,186m. Using 2,186m per PCS LiveStats.
Stage 1 start time: IDL says 13:25 CEST; PCS LiveStats shows 13:30. Using 13:25 per IDL (IDL tends to be more precise on race-day timing).

Prologue podium order 2nd/3rd: PCS says Söderqvist 2nd, Oliveira 3rd. AFP, CyclingUpToDate, IDL say Oliveira 2nd, Söderqvist 3rd. Cyclingnews main page says Söderqvist 2nd. Using PCS as authoritative. The difference is a few hundredths of a second.
Pogačar prologue finish position: AFP/The National say 5th; Cyclingnews result text says 6th; PCS page doesn't show the full standings. Using 5th based on AFP and The National.
Sosa stage win: 'First win since 2022' confirmed by multiple sources. His iliac artery (endofibrosis) surgery in 2025 confirmed by Spanish/Colombian cycling media and the Eurosport tweet.

Tour of Türkiye Stage 4 start time: Approximate. Stage 3 started 'at 12:31 local time' from Marmaris per infobae (Turkey is UTC+3, so 12:31 local = 09:31 UTC = 11:31 CEST). Using 11:30 CEST as Stage 4 approximate start from same city.
